title: Bengali numerals
body: Bengali numerals (Bengali: সংখ্যা, romanized: shôṅkha, Assamese: সংখ্যা, romanized: xoiŋkha, Meitei: মশীং; ꯃꯁꯤꯡ, romanized: mashing) are the units of the numeral system, originating from Bangladesh & Eastern Indian subcontinent, used officially in Bengali, as well as Assamese, and Manipuri. It is also used in Bishnupriya, Chakma and Hajong languages. They are used by more than 350 million people around the world and are a variety of the Hindu–Arabic numeral system.
categories: All Wikipedia articles written in Indian English
